settings.title: The One-Cloth Habit That Spreads More Grime
settings.excerpt: Using a single cloth around the whole house does not clean it, it just relocates the dirt.
settings.body:
The most common cleaning mistake we see in homes is also the simplest to fix: one cloth, one bucket, the whole house.

Wiping the bathroom and then the kitchen with the same cloth carries bacteria straight from one to the other. The surfaces look clean and are anything but, which is the worst sort of clean because you cannot see the problem you have created.

Use a colour for each zone

We keep separate cloths for bathrooms, kitchens, and general surfaces, and never cross them over. A cheap pack of coloured microfibres makes it foolproof, because the colour does the remembering for you. There is a reason professional kitchens and hospitals work the same way.

  • One colour for bathrooms
  • A second for kitchen surfaces
  • A third for dusting and general rooms

The same logic applies to your bucket and your sponges. Tipping grey water from the bathroom floor into the kitchen sink, or scrubbing both with one sponge, undoes the work. Fresh water and a clean cloth for each zone is a tiny habit that changes the result completely.

Rinse in hot water as you go, and wash them properly at the end. A microfibre cloth loaded with old grease just smears it around, so a hot wash without fabric softener keeps them grabbing dirt rather than pushing it. A clean cloth is the whole game.
